summary: Manual capture of cap_design-preferences-2026-05-05.md (4705 bytes). 10 extracts found across 9 type patterns.

extracted.text: ## Decision: The 5 mockup HTML files in docs/mockups/ are the canonical visual reference
extracted.rationale: ## Decision: The 5 mockup HTML files in docs/mockups/ are the canonical visual reference

`docs/mockups/workbench-night.html`, `docs/mockups/workbench-screens.html`, `docs/mockups/mockup-a-shay-shay-on-media-studio.html`, `docs/mockups/mockup-b-shays-workshop.html`, `docs/mockups/mockup-c-show-me-how.html` together comprise the canonical visual reference. Any rebuild of the Workbench shell must match these mockups visually before any architectural reorganization happens. They were preserved through the freeze on 2026-05-04 and are the source of truth for what the shell should look li

extracted.text: ## Anti-pattern: Building from design tokens without rebuilding from the screen mockups
extracted.rationale: ## Anti-pattern: Building from design tokens without rebuilding from the screen mockups

`docs/STUDIO-UI-FOUNDATION.md` Section 2 codifies the design tokens (color, type, glass, motion, glow). Section 8 codifies the workspace contract. But `docs/mockups/workbench-screens.html` shows 5 complete designed screens that the implementation never matched. The implementation took the tokens but invented its own layout. Anti-pattern: when both tokens and screen mockups exist, implementing from tokens alone produces a shell that is technically on-spec but visually nothing like the design. Alw

extracted.text: ## Gap: Workbench Foundation implementation diverged from designed mockups
extracted.rationale: ## Gap: Workbench Foundation implementation diverged from designed mockups

The current `site-studio/public/workbench-foundation.html` reproduces the Night Scheme color tokens from STUDIO-UI-FOUNDATION.md but does not match any of the 5 designed screens in `docs/mockups/workbench-screens.html`. Specifically: the home/portfolio screen is missing (mockup-screen-01 has the calm welcome, italic Fraunces hero, and warm site cards which never got built); the page editor screen is missing; the components library screen is missing; the media studio generation screen is missing;

extracted.text: ## Do-not-repeat: Do not invent middle-area layouts when mockups exist
extracted.rationale: ## Do-not-repeat: Do not invent middle-area layouts when mockups exist

extracted.text: When implementing a workbench domain, the implementor must render the matching mockup screen, not in
extracted.rationale: When implementing a workbench domain, the implementor must render the matching mockup screen, not invent its own layout. The current middle-area implementation (the macOS-styled Object/Sites/Page/Meta tile system) has no source in the mockups and was invented during build. Future builds: open the relevant mockup HTML side-by-side with the implementation and match it.

extracted.text: ## Rule: Per-page-type layout consideration is mandatory
extracted.rationale: ## Rule: Per-page-type layout consideration is mandatory

extracted.text: Different page types call for different layouts
extracted.rationale: Different page types call for different layouts. The 5 mockups demonstrate this: portfolio overview uses card grid, page editor uses sidebar+preview+sidebar, components library uses tile grid, media studio uses chat-with-grid+preview, Shay trace uses terminal log view. Future page additions must pick the right mockup pattern for the page type, not default to a one-size-fits-all template.
